page: Microsoft Azure D16ds_v4 is a general-purpose virtual machine belonging to the DDSv4 family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 16 dedicated vCPUs mapped from 8 physical Intel Xeon 8272CL cores with hyper-threading operating at 2.6 GHz. The processor includes a 35 MB L3 cache and supports instruction set extensions such as AVX, AVX-512, and AVX-512 VNNI. The system is equipped with 64.0 GB of RAM, providing a balanced 4.0 GB of memory per core.

This server includes 614 GB of local storage and is capable of utilizing Premium Storage. In benchmark testing, the D16ds_v4 demonstrates mid-range performance across multiple workloads. It achieves a single-core stress-ng CPU performance score of 1425.48 and a multi-core score of 14891.9. Memory bandwidth tests show a read-only small block cached speed of 831.03 GB/sec, which drops to 79.84 GB/sec for large uncached blocks, and a write speed of 45.97 GB/sec.

The hardware profile is suited for general-purpose workloads, database operations, and web serving. It handles static web serving at 12.0 GB/sec and processes 14.01 million Redis SET operations per second. For machine learning, the server processes small 135M LLM prompts at 743.24 tokens/sec, but performance drops significantly on larger models, with medium 7B model prompt processing falling to a weak tier of 24.63 tokens/sec. This highlights a clear tradeoff when attempting complex AI inference without dedicated GPU acceleration.
description: Microsoft Azure D16ds_v4 is a general-purpose virtual machine from the DDSv4 family, built on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 16 dedicated vCPUs powered by an Intel Xeon 8272CL processor running at 2.6 GHz, paired with 64.0 GB of RAM and 614 GB of local storage. The hardware architecture supports AVX-512 and AVX-512 VNNI instructions. Benchmark results place this server in the average tier for most compute and memory tasks, including a single-core Geekbench score of 1285.0 and a multi-core score of 7781.0. It delivers 12.0 GB/sec in static web serving and 14.01 million operations per second in Redis SET tests. However, it exhibits a performance bottleneck on larger workloads, such as medium-sized LLM inference, where prompt processing drops to a weak 24.63 tokens/sec. This configuration is suited for relational databases, web serving, and general-purpose enterprise applications.
og_description: Microsoft Azure D16ds_v4 is a general-purpose VM featuring 16 Intel Xeon vCPUs, 64 GB of RAM, and 614 GB of local storage, delivering balanced mid-range performance for databases and web serving.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure D16ds_v4 is a general-purpose VM with 16 Intel Xeon vCPUs, 64 GB RAM, and 614 GB local storage, optimized for balanced workloads.
tagline: A balanced general-purpose server featuring dedicated Intel Xeon processors, local storage, and consistent mid-range performance for enterprise workloads.
bullet_points: 16 dedicated vCPUs and 64 GB RAM providing a balanced 1:4 resource ratio
614 GB of local storage with Premium Storage capability
Intel Xeon 8272CL processor with AVX-512 instruction set support
Average-tier performance in database operations and static web serving
Tradeoffs in machine learning, showing weak performance on medium LLM models
